message:
Ever notice your dog doing slow, careful laps before finally settling on the *perfect* spot to do their business? Turns out there may be more to it than plain fussiness. 🐶🧭

In a two-year study, researchers observed thousands of bathroom breaks and found that dogs prefer to line their bodies up along the north–south axis of Earth's magnetic field — and actively avoid pointing east–west. The pattern only showed up when the planet's magnetic field was calm and steady, which may be why it took so long for anyone to spot it.

Scientists still aren't entirely sure WHY they do it, but it's one more hint that our pups can sense the magnetic world in ways we're only beginning to understand. 🌍

So next time your dog takes forever to "find the spot," cut them a little slack — they might just be checking their built-in compass. 😄

Does your dog have a funny pre-potty ritual? Tell me about it in the comments! 🐾
